title: prunella vulgaris subsp. vulgaris
intro: Prunella vulgaris subsp. vulgaris, also known as the common self-heal or heal-all, is a perennial herbaceous plant in the mint family, native to Europe, Asia, and North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Prunella vulgaris subsp. vulgaris include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and aid in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Prunella vulgaris subsp. vulgaris can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies in the form of salves, ointments, and poultices.
